instructions.title: Instructions
instructions.subtitle: Everything you need to know to get the most out of Lucrato every day.
instructions.sec1Body:
The system is organized into dedicated tabs for each function:
• Inventory — daily executive overview with KPIs and alerts. Always start here.
• Purchases — where you record each product batch. One row per purchase.
• Sales — where you record each sale. One row per sale. The same product can have several sales at different prices.
• Dashboard — consolidated charts and indicators.
• Analytics — summaries by product, category, month, and idle stock.
• Settings — fees, alert days, lists, and editable parameters.
• Instructions — this guide.
instructions.sec2Body:
1. Go to Purchases and click "New Purchase".
2. Fill in:
   • Batch ID: unique code (e.g., C011). Use a new code for each purchase, even if the product is the same.
   • Product, category, supplier, date.
   • Quantity and unit cost.
   • Shipping and other purchase costs (optional).
3. The calculated fields (real total cost, real unit cost, current stock) appear automatically.
instructions.sec3Body:
1. Go to Sales and click "New Sale".
2. Fill in:
   • Sale ID: unique code (e.g., V005).
   • Batch ID: select the matching batch — this pulls the correct cost automatically.
   • Quantity sold, unit price, date, channel.
   • ML fee: comes pre-filled with the default. Edit it if the fee was different for this sale.
   • Seller shipping, discount, and other costs (optional).
3. The system calculates net revenue, profit, and margin in real time.
instructions.sec4Body:
Create a new row in Sales for each sale, even if it's the same product. Each sale has its own profit and margin calculation.

Example: batch C005 with 3 units at R$ 98,45.
• V010 | C005 | 1 unit | R$ 220,00 → margin calculated individually
• V011 | C005 | 1 unit | R$ 180,00 → margin calculated individually
• V012 | C005 | 1 unit | R$ 195,00 → margin calculated individually

The cost (R$ 98,45) is pulled automatically into all three rows via the Batch ID.
instructions.sec5Body:
The Batch ID connects purchases and sales. Without it filled in correctly, cost calculations go wrong.

• Bought the same product again? Create a new ID (e.g., C011). Each batch has its own cost.
• Same product from different suppliers? Two different IDs.
• Always select the Batch ID when recording a sale — it's in the dropdown on the Sales tab.
instructions.sec6Title: How to read the Inventory tab
instructions.sec6Body:
The Inventory tab is your executive overview. Nothing needs to be filled in here.

Key KPIs:
• Total Invested — all the money spent buying products.
• Idle Capital — Stock × Real Unit Cost. Money locked in product.
• Gross / Net Revenue — total billed and what you actually receive.
• Net Profit — real result after fees, shipping, and costs.
• Net Margin — sales efficiency.

Batch status:
• Sold: batch emptied.
• In Stock: within the normal window.
• Attention: idle for ≥25 days. Consider lowering the price.
• Stale: idle for ≥30 days. Urgent action.
instructions.sec7Body:
On the Purchases tab, the "Purchase Date" column changes color automatically:
• Yellow: stock > 0 and between 25 and 29 days since purchase.
• Red: stock > 0 and ≥30 days since purchase.
• No color: stock = 0 (sold).

The day thresholds are configurable in Settings.
instructions.sec8Body:
On the Settings tab you can adjust:
• Default ML fee (12% by default).
• Days for yellow (25) and red (30) alerts.
• Desired minimum net margin (10%).
• Default purchase shipping.
• Lists of categories, suppliers, and sales channels.

Remember to click "Save" after changing.
instructions.sec9Body:
Bought a product?
→ Purchases tab → "New Purchase" → fill in → done.

Made a sale?
→ Sales tab → "New Sale" → correct Batch ID → price and quantity → done.
→ Stock on the Purchases tab and the overview on the Inventory tab update automatically.

Weekly review (5 minutes):
1. Inventory tab → check KPIs and alerts.
2. Yellow/red dates → products that need attention.
3. Orange/red margins in Sales → sales below expectations.
4. Dashboard → revenue and profit trends.
instructions.sec10Body:
• Always fill in the Batch ID on sales — without it, the cost is zero and profit inflated.
• Use new IDs for new purchases of the same product.
• The ML fee can vary per sale — edit it in the form when needed.
• Use Settings → Bulk Import to import purchases and sales via an Excel spreadsheet.
• Your data is saved in the cloud and accessible from any device after login.
• You can switch between light and dark theme at any time using the button in the top-right corner.

auth.heroTagline: The financial dashboard for Mercado Livre sellers.
auth.heroSub: Serious as a bank statement. Alive as an investing app. Honest as a well-made spreadsheet.
auth.heroBullet1: Net profit, idle capital, and margins in real time
auth.heroBullet2: Stale-stock alerts before they turn into losses
auth.heroBullet3: Excel import and CSV export for your accountant
